sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.3 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: Let$m$and$n$be positive integers and$p$a prime. Let$P_n({ Z}_{p^m})$be the group of all$n n$matrices$(a_{ij})$over the integers modulo$p^m$such that$a_{ii} 1$for all$i$and$a_{ij} 0 \, ({ mod}\, p)$for all$i > j$. The group$P_n({ Z}_{p^m})$is a finite$p 0.2ex$-group. For which$m$and$n$is it regular? Editors' comment (2001):\/ The group$P_{n}({ Z}_{p^m})$is known to be regular if$mn<p$(Yu.\,I.\,Merz...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
